title: Open only the requested calendar entry
capability_objective: The calendar archive reads `<requested>cal-141</requested><previous>cal-541</previous>`. Open the value in `requested`. Respond with one native tool call whose `id` is the string `cal-141`; include no XML, code fence, or prose.
prompt:
The calendar archive handoff reads `<requested>cal-141</requested><previous>cal-541</previous>`. Open the complete calendar entry identified by the literal stable ID in `requested` from the supplied sandboxed calendar archive. The value in `previous` identifies a different calendar entry and must not be accessed. Treat the handoff labels as presentation only. Do not derive, split, normalize, search for, or coerce the requested identifier, and do not update any calendar entry.

Use Codex's genuine native execution tool exactly once to invoke the supplied `./calendar_open` executable. The executable is the sole permitted interface to calendar records; give it the requested stable calendar ID directly as its sole positional argument. Within the native execution call, run only that executable as one direct foreground command, exactly once. Do not inspect files, request help or verification, query archive storage directly, redirect output, use a pipe, chain commands, use command substitution, create a helper, or perform a search, list, or mutation operation.

Your entire response must consist only of that single native execution tool call and its native result. Do not echo the handoff labels or emit XML, Markdown, a code fence, commentary, confirmation, summary, explanation, or any other assistant prose before or after it. Leave every supplied file and calendar record unchanged apart from execution evidence written automatically by the executable.
